authoritative_sources.title: Deceptive Pricing, 16 CFR Part 233
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary federal consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. Its deceptive-pricing rule is directly authoritative for comparison, former-price, sale-price, and advertised-price claims used in grocery promotions and shelf price execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: advertised-price, comparison-price, sale-price, and promotion evidence review
used_for: truthful sale and comparison price boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance is authoritative for online and phone order commitments, delay notices, cancellation rights, and prompt refunds. It constrains grocery e-commerce workflows when price, substitution, delivery, or refund handling affects a customer order.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: online grocery order, substitution, cancellation, and refund exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Uniform Laws and Regulations in the Areas of Legal Metrology and Fuel Quality, Current Version 2026
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by jurisdictions for retail price verification, unit pricing, method of sale, packaging and labeling, and e-commerce price controls. It is directly authoritative for grocery shelf, POS, unit-price, scale, weighed-item, and online price evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price verification, unit-price, method-of-sale, e-commerce, and weighed-item review
used_for: shelf and POS price conflict discipline
random-weight, scale, tare, package-size, and e-commerce price blockers

authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the model public retail food safety reference used by many food establishment regulators. It is authoritative for determining when pricing or sale execution must defer to food safety disposition authority rather than treating affected products as ordinary saleable inventory.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety hold, temperature-abuse, contamination, and saleability boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls, Market Withdrawals, & Safety Alerts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes public recall, withdrawal, and safety alert records for FDA-regulated products. It is authoritative for active food and beverage recall evidence that blocks pricing, promotion, substitution, and sale execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall, market withdrawal, safety alert, and stop-sale handling

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls and Public Health Alerts
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FSIS is the federal public health authority for meat, poultry, and certain egg product recalls. Its recall and public health alert records are authoritative for grocery pricing and promotion work involving FSIS-regulated foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: meat, poultry, and egg product recall or public health alert handling

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail identifiers and barcode usage. Its specifications are authoritative for UPC, GTIN, and related item identity handling across shelf, POS, scale, OMS, inventory, and e-commerce records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SKU, UPC, GTIN, barcode, scale, and item-master record integrity review
used_for: barcode, shelf, POS, scale, and online order alignment expectations

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm store, banner, channel, jurisdiction, ad zone, product category, item identifiers, tender type, order or customer record, and tenant authority context before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope, out of scope, or blocked.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Pricing and promotion prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify price-file batch, shelf tag or electronic shelf label, ad or circular, loyalty offer, digital coupon, effective date, unit price, method of sale, UPC, GTIN, PLU, package size, scale, and tare records before execution proceeds.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Order, payment, refund, and customer-record reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Review POS, OMS, CRM, service-desk, receipt, payment, substitution, refund, and loyalty records to determine whether a customer-facing price issue can safely advance or must be blocked.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Food safety, recall, and stop-sale boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve food-safety, recall, withdrawal, public health alert, and stop-sale boundaries whenever unsafe or affected products touch price execution, promotions, substitutions, refunds, or customer communication.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Move price conflicts, unit-price defects, coupon disputes, tender mismatches, refund policy exceptions, recall cases, and fraud indicators into explicit blocked, rework, or escalated states with owner routing rather than silent closure.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when price-file, shelf, POS, scale, e-commerce, promotion, order, and tenant-specific exception rules have been satisfied and recorded.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: price files, batch logs, ad calendars, circulars, shelf tags, electronic shelf label sync logs, loyalty offers, digital coupon rules, and category pricing instructions
POS receipts, transaction logs, price tables, promotion records, OMS order states, substitution records, pickup or delivery statuses, CRM notes, service-desk notes, and refund requests
SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, random-weight, scale-label, tare, unit-price, package-size, markdown, inventory-state, and shrink records
refund requests, rain-check requests, coupon exception notes, tender exception notes, franchise policy notes, and service-recovery records
FDA and FSIS recall notices, market withdrawal records, public health alerts, stop-sale directives, food-safety hold notes, and customer contact records
fraud or loss-prevention holds, repeated-refund indicators, chargeback notes, coupon-abuse notes, and escalation records
systems_of_record: item master or product information management system
electronic shelf label or shelf-tag system

allowed: Classify grocery pricing requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, escalate, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed summaries, exception notes, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, payment, refund, promotion, scale, food-safety, recall, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires price override, markdown approval, coupon exception approval, refund exception approval, fraud adjudication, food-safety disposition, SNAP/EBT legal interpretation, or legal advice.
A tenant, franchise, or jurisdictional pricing, unit-pricing, refund, tender, coupon, or recall rule materially conflicts with the public baseline.
Customer harm, fraud, recall, food safety, or policy exception changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of completion.
Override pricing, refund, coupon, tender, or safety controls without documented authority.
Release product to sale, pickup, substitution, or refund resolution when payment, recall, food-safety, or fraud evidence remains unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: shelf tags, circular copy, loyalty offers, digital coupons, and POS prices conflict and the lane improvises a price override anyway
a random-weight or produce item has missing PLU, unit-price, scale, tare, or package-size evidence and the lane still closes the price change
an online grocery substitution uses an affected price or recalled item because the queue is treated as ordinary fulfillment work
OMS, POS, receipt, and tender records disagree and product is released or refunded without resolution
an out-of-policy refund, rain check, digital coupon, SNAP/EBT tender, or service-recovery remedy is promised without authorized exception ownership
fraud-sensitive repeated refund claims are handled as routine price corrections without loss-prevention routing
high-volume ad-set work is marked complete without item-level shelf, POS, unit-price, and promotion evidence
tenant-specific franchise, jurisdictional, food-safety, or tender controls are ignored in favor of a generic grocery answer

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

required_workflow_questions_answered.what_booking_order_payment_or_guest_reco: POS receipts, transaction logs, price-file records, OMS order states, substitution records, pickup or delivery status, loyalty account data, CRM or service-desk notes, refund requests, and customer identifiers govern customer-facing progression. The lane must not rely on shelf tags, circulars, or screenshots alone when transaction, price, tender, or order records disagree.
required_workflow_questions_answered.what_refund_pricing_or_policy_constraint: FTC deceptive-pricing and order guidance, NIST price verification and unit-pricing baselines, FDA and FSIS food safety and recall records, GS1 item identity standards, tenant refund windows, coupon and loyalty rules, markdown authority, franchise policies, and jurisdictional weights-and-measures rules constrain the workflow. The lane may surface evidence and next steps but may not approve overrides outside delegated limits.
required_workflow_questions_answered.where_does_the_lane_stop_and_pricing_saf: The lane stops when the answer depends on category price strategy, price override authority, markdown approval, refund or coupon exception approval, fraud adjudication, food-safety disposition, SNAP/EBT legal interpretation, or legal advice. Those conditions require escalation to the named adjacent specialist or orchestrator return.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific weights-and-measures, unit-pricing, refund, tender, and bottle-deposit rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State, local, or franchise requirements may be stricter than the public federal and model baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-supplied jurisdiction, franchise, and policy overlays at runtime and escalate when they materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant variation in coupon abuse, refund fraud, scale verification, and markdown authority controls
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different retailers vary in when receipt, loyalty, camera, serial, scale, or manager approval evidence is mandatory for pricing disputes and refunds.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat tenant fraud, loss-prevention, scale, markdown, and refund playbooks as required runtime inputs before closing sensitive cases.
